project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project is BEST defined as which of the following?
options: An ongoing operational function with a permanent team
A temporary endeavor undertaken to create a unique product, service, or result
A recurring annual process managed by a standing committee
A continuous improvement program with no defined end date
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: A temporary endeavor undertaken to create a unique product, service, or result

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: The triple constraint of project management refers to:
options: People, process, and technology
Scope, schedule, and cost – balanced against quality
Strategy, execution, and governance
Planning, monitoring, and closing
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Scope, schedule, and cost – balanced against quality

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project baseline is MOST accurately described as:
options: The original project charter before any revisions
The approved version of a project plan component used as a reference point for measuring performance
The project schedule before stakeholder review
The final deliverable submitted to the sponsor
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: The approved version of a project plan component used as a reference point for measuring performance

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: Which document formally authorizes a project and grants the project manager authority to apply organizational resources?
options: Project management plan
Statement of work
Project charter
Risk register
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Project charter

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project's 'deliverable' is BEST described as:
options: A task assigned to a team member
Any unique, verifiable product, result, or capability produced as an output of a project activity
A milestone with no associated work
A project status report
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Any unique, verifiable product, result, or capability produced as an output of a project activity

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: Which statement BEST describes the difference between a project and a program?
options: Programs are smaller in scope than projects
A program is a group of related projects managed in a coordinated way to obtain benefits not available from managing them individually
Projects are always part of a program
Programs do not require a charter
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: A program is a group of related projects managed in a coordinated way to obtain benefits not available from managing them individually

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project's 'critical path' is defined as:
options: The sequence of tasks with the most resources assigned
The sequence of scheduled activities that determines the longest path through the project, defining the minimum project duration
The path with the most risk
The path connecting all milestones
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: The sequence of scheduled activities that determines the longest path through the project, defining the minimum project duration

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: 'Float' (or slack) in project scheduling refers to:
options: The contingency budget reserved for risk events
The amount of time a task can be delayed without delaying the project's completion date or a successor task's early start
The number of unassigned resources on the project
Buffer time added arbitrarily to each task
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: The amount of time a task can be delayed without delaying the project's completion date or a successor task's early start

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: In Critical Path Method (CPM), an activity with zero float:
options: Can be delayed without affecting the project end date
Has no dependencies
Lies on the critical path and any delay will delay the project
Is a risk that has been mitigated
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Lies on the critical path and any delay will delay the project

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project has four tasks: A (5 days, no predecessor), B (3 days, follows A), C (7 days, follows A), D (2 days, follows B and C). What is the critical path duration?
options: 10 days
12 days
14 days
9 days
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: 14 days

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: The Program Evaluation and Review Technique (PERT) differs from CPM primarily in that PERT:
options: Only uses one time estimate per task
Uses three time estimates (optimistic, most likely, pessimistic) to address schedule uncertainty
Does not identify the critical path
Is used only for construction projects
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Uses three time estimates (optimistic, most likely, pessimistic) to address schedule uncertainty

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: Using PERT, a task has an optimistic estimate of 4 days, a most likely estimate of 6 days, and a pessimistic estimate of 14 days. What is the PERT expected duration?
options: 6 days
7 days
8 days
9 days
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: 7 days

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A 'Finish-to-Start' (FS) dependency with a lag of 3 days means:
options: The successor task must finish 3 days after the predecessor finishes
The successor task cannot start until 3 days after the predecessor finishes
The successor task must finish 3 days before the predecessor starts
The predecessor task must wait 3 days before starting
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: The successor task cannot start until 3 days after the predecessor finishes

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: Fast tracking a project schedule means:
options: Replacing all tasks with automated processes
Performing phases or activities in parallel that were originally planned sequentially, accepting increased risk
Adding more resources to reduce task durations
Eliminating non-critical tasks from the schedule
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Performing phases or activities in parallel that were originally planned sequentially, accepting increased risk

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: Crashing a project schedule involves:
options: Eliminating scope to meet the deadline
Adding resources (often at increased cost) to reduce the duration of critical path activities
Removing non-critical activities from the plan
Extending the project deadline
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Adding resources (often at increased cost) to reduce the duration of critical path activities

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A Gantt chart PRIMARILY communicates:
options: Project budget and cost variances
Task dependencies using network logic only
The project schedule showing tasks, durations, and time periods in bar chart format
Risk probability and impact scores
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: The project schedule showing tasks, durations, and time periods in bar chart format

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A Request for Proposal (RFP) differs from a Request for Quotation (RFQ) in that an RFP:
options: Requests a fixed price for a commodity product with defined specifications
Invites vendors to propose solutions and methodologies for a more complex requirement where the solution is not fully defined
Is only used for construction projects
Requires no vendor response timeline
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Invites vendors to propose solutions and methodologies for a more complex requirement where the solution is not fully defined

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: Which evaluation criterion is MOST important when assessing vendor bids for a complex IT project?
options: Alphabetical order of vendor names
A weighted scoring matrix that evaluates technical approach, past performance, staffing, and cost against defined requirements
The vendor's geographic proximity to the agency
The vendor's marketing materials
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: A weighted scoring matrix that evaluates technical approach, past performance, staffing, and cost against defined requirements

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project manager receives two bids: Vendor A at $500,000 with a 10-month timeline and Vendor B at $350,000 with an 18-month timeline. The project has a hard deadline in 12 months and the cost of delay is $80,000/month. Which vendor provides better value?
options: Vendor B because the initial cost is lower
Vendor A because it meets the deadline and avoids $480,000 in potential delay costs
Vendor B because vendors should always be selected on price
Neither vendor meets the requirements
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Vendor A because it meets the deadline and avoids $480,000 in potential delay costs

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A Statement of Work (SOW) in a procurement context MUST include which essential element?
options: The vendor's internal organizational chart
A precise description of the work to be performed, deliverables, and acceptance criteria
The project manager's biography
A list of past projects unrelated to the current contract
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: A precise description of the work to be performed, deliverables, and acceptance criteria

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: During bid evaluation, a vendor's response is technically superior but 40% more expensive than the next best proposal. A project manager should:
options: Automatically select the lower-cost vendor
Conduct a cost-benefit analysis to determine whether the technical superiority justifies the additional cost relative to project risk
Select the higher-cost vendor without analysis
Request that both vendors lower their prices to the same level
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Conduct a cost-benefit analysis to determine whether the technical superiority justifies the additional cost relative to project risk

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: Earned Value Management (EVM) integrates which three dimensions of project performance?
options: Quality, risk, and stakeholder satisfaction
Planned value (PV), earned value (EV), and actual cost (AC)
Scope, resources, and procurement
Budget, schedule, and communications
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Planned value (PV), earned value (EV), and actual cost (AC)

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project has a Planned Value (PV) of $100,000, an Earned Value (EV) of $80,000, and an Actual Cost (AC) of $90,000. What is the Schedule Performance Index (SPI)?
Using the same data as the previous question (PV=$100K, EV=$80K, AC=$90K), what is the Cost Performance Index (CPI)?
A project with a CPI of 0.85 and an SPI of 0.92 indicates which condition?
options: The project is over budget and behind schedule
The project is under budget and ahead of schedule
The project is on budget but behind schedule
The project is over budget and ahead of schedule
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: The project is over budget and behind schedule

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project status report shows a Schedule Variance (SV) of -$15,000. This means:
options: The project has spent $15,000 less than planned
The project has accomplished $15,000 less work than planned at this point in time
The project is $15,000 over budget
The schedule has been compressed by $15,000
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: The project has accomplished $15,000 less work than planned at this point in time

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: Which project monitoring artifact provides the MOST comprehensive view of project health across scope, schedule, cost, and risk simultaneously?
options: Gantt chart
Project status dashboard with integrated KPIs
Risk register alone
Invoice tracking spreadsheet
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Project status dashboard with integrated KPIs

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project manager holds weekly status meetings with a distributed team. Which practice MOST effectively ensures accountability for action items?
options: Rely on team members to remember their commitments verbally
Document action items with owner, due date, and status; distribute minutes; and review open items at the start of each subsequent meeting
Send a summary email without assigning owners
Hold more frequent meetings until all items are resolved
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Document action items with owner, due date, and status; distribute minutes; and review open items at the start of each subsequent meeting

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project manager identifies that a deliverable is three weeks behind schedule. What is the FIRST step?
options: Immediately notify the sponsor
Perform root cause analysis to understand why the delay occurred before determining corrective action
Add resources to the task without further analysis
Revise the baseline schedule to hide the variance
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Perform root cause analysis to understand why the delay occurred before determining corrective action

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: Which document records all project changes, their status, and disposition decisions?
options: Risk register
Change log
Issues log
Communications plan
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Change log

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project manager receives a request for a small change that 'won't take more than an hour.' The CORRECT response is to:
options: Approve and implement it immediately to maintain goodwill
Route it through the formal change control process to assess cumulative impact
Deny all small changes to prevent scope creep
Implement it informally and document it in the next status report
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Route it through the formal change control process to assess cumulative impact

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: Scope creep on a project is BEST managed through:
options: Accepting all stakeholder requests to maintain relationships
A formal integrated change control process with impact analysis and sponsor approval
Reducing the project team to limit intake of new requests
Increasing the project budget to accommodate all changes
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: A formal integrated change control process with impact analysis and sponsor approval

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project manager discovers that a task's actual duration has exceeded its planned duration. The FIRST corrective action should be to:
options: Immediately crash the critical path
Assess whether the overrun affects the critical path and project end date, then evaluate options
Revise the project charter
Notify all stakeholders simultaneously
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Assess whether the overrun affects the critical path and project end date, then evaluate options

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: Resource leveling is used to:
options: Reduce project cost by eliminating resources
Resolve resource conflicts by adjusting the schedule to work within resource constraints, which may extend the project duration
Increase the number of resources assigned to all tasks
Eliminate the critical path
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Resolve resource conflicts by adjusting the schedule to work within resource constraints, which may extend the project duration

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A milestone on a project schedule represents:
options: A task requiring more than 40 hours of effort
A significant point or event in a project, typically with zero duration
A task assigned to the project manager
Any task on the critical path
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: A significant point or event in a project, typically with zero duration

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project has a 10-week baseline schedule. After 5 weeks, 40% of the work is complete. The project is:
options: On schedule
Ahead of schedule
Behind schedule – 10% less work has been completed than planned
At risk due to budget overruns only
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Behind schedule – 10% less work has been completed than planned

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: Which technique involves breaking down the project scope into progressively smaller components until work can be reliably estimated and assigned?
options: Critical path analysis
Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) decomposition
Risk breakdown structure
Organizational breakdown structure
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) decomposition

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) should be decomposed to which level?
options: Until every individual keystroke is documented
To the work package level, where work can be reliably estimated, assigned, and monitored
Until only one team member is responsible for each element
Until the project charter is complete
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: To the work package level, where work can be reliably estimated, assigned, and monitored

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project retrospective (lessons learned session) is conducted PRIMARILY to:
options: Assign blame for project failures
Capture what went well and what should be improved so future projects benefit from current experience
Finalize the project budget
Renegotiate contracts with vendors
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Capture what went well and what should be improved so future projects benefit from current experience

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: Project closure activities include which of the following?
options: Initiating a new project immediately without documentation
Obtaining formal acceptance of deliverables, archiving project documents, releasing resources, and conducting lessons learned
Reassigning all project team members without documentation
Destroying project records to protect confidentiality
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Obtaining formal acceptance of deliverables, archiving project documents, releasing resources, and conducting lessons learned

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project is deemed successful when:
options: It was delivered under budget regardless of scope
All project objectives are met within the agreed constraints and stakeholders accept the deliverables
The project team enjoyed working on it
It was completed ahead of schedule regardless of cost
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: All project objectives are met within the agreed constraints and stakeholders accept the deliverables

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: Post-implementation review differs from a lessons learned session in that a post-implementation review:
options: Is conducted during project planning
Evaluates whether the solution delivered the expected business benefits after a period of operational use
Is only conducted for failed projects
Focuses exclusively on budget performance
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Evaluates whether the solution delivered the expected business benefits after a period of operational use

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A qualitative risk assessment matrix ranks risks based on:
options: Actual financial cost of each risk
Probability of occurrence and potential impact on project objectives
Number of stakeholders affected by the risk
Technical complexity of the risk mitigation strategy
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Probability of occurrence and potential impact on project objectives

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: Which risk response strategy involves working with a third party to share the financial impact of a risk event?
options: Avoid
Mitigate
Transfer
Accept
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Transfer

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project manager identifies a risk with a 30% probability and a $50,000 impact. What is the risk's expected monetary value (EMV)?
A risk that has already occurred is reclassified as:
options: A residual risk
An issue
A secondary risk
A trigger
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: An issue

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A 'risk trigger' is BEST described as:
options: The financial value of a risk
An indicator or warning sign that a risk event is about to occur
The person responsible for monitoring a risk
The probability score assigned to a risk
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: An indicator or warning sign that a risk event is about to occur

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: Which risk response strategy eliminates the threat entirely by changing the project plan to remove the risk?
options: Transfer
Mitigate
Accept
Avoid
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Avoid

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: Residual risks are:
options: Risks that have been fully mitigated
Risks that remain after risk response strategies have been implemented
New risks identified during project execution
Risks transferred to a vendor
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Risks that remain after risk response strategies have been implemented

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A stakeholder has HIGH interest in the project but LOW power to influence decisions. The appropriate engagement strategy is to:
options: Ignore them since they lack power
Keep them informed with regular updates and involve them in relevant decisions
Give them full decision-making authority
Limit all communications to the project sponsor
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Keep them informed with regular updates and involve them in relevant decisions

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A communications management plan defines which of the following for each communication?
options: Developer assignments
What information is communicated, to whom, when, by what method, and who is responsible
The project budget allocation
Technical architecture specifications
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: What information is communicated, to whom, when, by what method, and who is responsible

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project manager needs to communicate an unexpected $200,000 cost overrun to the project sponsor. Which communication approach is MOST appropriate?
options: Send an email and hope the sponsor responds
Deliver the message in a face-to-face meeting, presenting root cause analysis, options, and a recommended corrective action plan
Include it only in the monthly status report without explanation
Delay communication until the issue resolves itself
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Deliver the message in a face-to-face meeting, presenting root cause analysis, options, and a recommended corrective action plan

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project is at 75% completion with a Budget at Completion (BAC) of $400,000, an Actual Cost (AC) of $330,000, and an Earned Value (EV) of $300,000. What is the Estimate at Completion (EAC) using the CPI-based formula?
A project using a fixed-price contract experiences scope changes that the vendor argues are outside the original statement of work. The project manager should FIRST:
options: Pay the vendor's requested increase immediately
Review the original contract SOW, assess whether the change is in or out of scope, and engage legal/procurement if needed before approving any additional payment
Deny all vendor claims regardless of merit
Issue a stop-work order immediately
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Review the original contract SOW, assess whether the change is in or out of scope, and engage legal/procurement if needed before approving any additional payment

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: Which scheduling technique allows the project manager to perform 'what-if' analysis on how changes to one task affect the overall project timeline?
options: Resource leveling
Schedule network analysis using CPM/PERT tools
Gantt chart review
Issue log review
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Schedule network analysis using CPM/PERT tools

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project plan shows Task A (5 days) must finish before Task B (3 days) can start, and Task C (4 days) can start when Task A is 50% complete. What is the EARLIEST possible finish for all three tasks if Task A starts on Day 1?
options: Day 8
Day 9
Day 10
Day 11
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Day 8

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project manager learns that a critical team member will be unavailable for two weeks during a critical phase. The BEST immediate action is to:
options: Remove the critical tasks from scope
Assess the schedule impact, identify alternative resources or task resequencing, and update the risk register
Do nothing until the absence occurs
Extend the project deadline by two weeks immediately
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Assess the schedule impact, identify alternative resources or task resequencing, and update the risk register

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: Which project management process group focuses on defining and authorizing the project or project phase?
options: Planning
Executing
Monitoring and Controlling
Initiating
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Initiating

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A change control board (CCB) is PRIMARILY responsible for:
options: Writing the project charter
Reviewing, evaluating, approving, or rejecting change requests to the project baseline
Managing day-to-day project tasks
Selecting the project manager
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Reviewing, evaluating, approving, or rejecting change requests to the project baseline

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project manager identifies that the current trajectory will cause the project to miss its deadline by three weeks. The sponsor insists the deadline is fixed. Which option is MOST viable without scope reduction?
options: Accept the delay
Crash the schedule by adding resources to critical path tasks, accepting increased cost
Eliminate quality review processes
Ask the sponsor to replace key team members
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Crash the schedule by adding resources to critical path tasks, accepting increased cost

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project's Schedule Performance Index (SPI) is 1.15. This means:
options: The project is over budget
The project is 15% behind schedule
The project is performing 15% ahead of schedule based on the work accomplished
The project is 15% over its cost baseline
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: The project is performing 15% ahead of schedule based on the work accomplished

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: Which document captures the work packages, tasks, and hierarchy of all project deliverables in a structured format?
options: Risk register
Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) dictionary
Network diagram
Communications plan
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) dictionary

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project team is using an iterative project management approach (e.g., Scrum). Sprint reviews serve which primary monitoring function?
options: Tracking vendor invoice payments
Demonstrating completed work to stakeholders and gathering feedback that informs the next sprint's backlog
Performing risk analysis for the next fiscal year
Revising the project charter
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Demonstrating completed work to stakeholders and gathering feedback that informs the next sprint's backlog

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: When preparing a bid response for a government IT project, a vendor's proposed staffing plan should be evaluated against which criterion FIRST?
options: The number of resumes submitted
Whether proposed staff have relevant qualifications and demonstrated experience matched to the specific roles required by the SOW
The vendor's headquarters location
Whether proposed staff are local to the agency
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Whether proposed staff have relevant qualifications and demonstrated experience matched to the specific roles required by the SOW

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A 'rolling wave planning' approach is BEST described as:
options: Planning the entire project in detail before starting any work
Elaborating near-term work in detail while planning future phases at a high level, with detail added progressively as more information becomes available
Repeating the same plan for each project phase
Using only top-down estimation techniques
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Elaborating near-term work in detail while planning future phases at a high level, with detail added progressively as more information becomes available

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project manager's role during project execution is PRIMARILY to:
options: Design the technical architecture
Direct and manage project work, monitor performance, manage risks and issues, and communicate with stakeholders
Write code for the development team
Conduct user acceptance testing
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Direct and manage project work, monitor performance, manage risks and issues, and communicate with stakeholders

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: An assumption log in project management is used to:
options: Document all risk events that have occurred
Track all assumptions made during planning, monitor their validity throughout the project, and document the impact if assumptions prove false
Record team member vacation schedules
List all approved project changes
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Track all assumptions made during planning, monitor their validity throughout the project, and document the impact if assumptions prove false

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: Which tool BEST supports identifying the project tasks required to produce each deliverable and establishing their logical sequence?
options: Responsibility assignment matrix (RAM)
Precedence diagramming method (PDM)
Budget variance report
Stakeholder register
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Precedence diagramming method (PDM)

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project sponsor asks for the Estimate to Complete (ETC) for a project with a BAC of $500,000, an EV of $200,000, an AC of $240,000, and a CPI of 0.833. Using the CPI-adjusted formula, what is the ETC?
'Scope verification' (validate scope) in project management is concerned with:
options: Confirming requirements were gathered correctly
Obtaining formal stakeholder acceptance of completed project deliverables
Verifying that the project schedule is accurate
Confirming that all test cases have passed
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Obtaining formal stakeholder acceptance of completed project deliverables

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project manager must reduce a 20-week project to 15 weeks. Analysis shows that crashing will cost $10,000 per week saved on critical tasks but fast tracking introduces a 25% rework risk on affected tasks. The project manager should:
options: Automatically choose crashing
Automatically choose fast tracking
Analyze cost, risk, and schedule trade-offs for each option and select the approach that best balances project objectives
Do nothing and accept the 20-week timeline
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Analyze cost, risk, and schedule trade-offs for each option and select the approach that best balances project objectives

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: During project closure, all of the following are required EXCEPT:
options: Formal stakeholder acceptance of deliverables
Archiving project documents
Releasing project team members
Initiating the next project's requirements elicitation
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Initiating the next project's requirements elicitation

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A RACI matrix assigns 'Responsible' to the person who:
options: Is ultimately accountable for the outcome
Does the actual work to complete the task
Provides expert input and must be consulted
Receives information about the task outcome
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Does the actual work to complete the task

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: Which type of project cost estimate has the WIDEST acceptable variance range and is prepared with the LEAST information?
options: Definitive estimate
Parametric estimate
Analogous (rough order of magnitude) estimate
Bottom-up estimate
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Analogous (rough order of magnitude) estimate

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project has 5 stakeholders. How many potential communication channels exist?
When a project manager detects a consistent pattern of schedule slippage across multiple tasks, the MOST likely root cause to investigate FIRST is:
options: Individual team member performance failures
Systemic issues such as inadequate resource allocation, unrealistic estimates, or unmanaged dependencies
Sponsor disengagement
Vendor non-performance
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Systemic issues such as inadequate resource allocation, unrealistic estimates, or unmanaged dependencies

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: An issues log differs from a risk register in that an issues log records:
options: Future uncertainties and their mitigation strategies
Problems or matters that have already materialized and require resolution
Approved change requests
Completed project deliverables
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Problems or matters that have already materialized and require resolution

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project manager is told that the project budget cannot be increased. Two weeks before deadline, a critical vendor deliverable is late. The project manager should:
options: Cancel the project
Renegotiate the vendor contract, assess alternative sourcing, evaluate scope adjustments, and escalate to the sponsor with options and recommendations
Wait for the vendor to deliver and accept the delay passively
Hire additional internal staff immediately beyond the approved budget
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Renegotiate the vendor contract, assess alternative sourcing, evaluate scope adjustments, and escalate to the sponsor with options and recommendations

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: In project risk management, an 'opportunity' is:
options: A type of schedule variance
A positive risk event that, if it occurs, would benefit the project
A change request submitted by a stakeholder
A task completed ahead of schedule
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: A positive risk event that, if it occurs, would benefit the project

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project management office (PMO) in a New York State agency context MOST likely provides which function?
options: Writing code for all agency IT projects
Setting project management standards, providing governance oversight, and offering portfolio-level visibility to leadership
Managing only vendor relationships
Replacing the role of individual project managers
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Setting project management standards, providing governance oversight, and offering portfolio-level visibility to leadership

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: When presenting a project status to an executive steering committee, the project manager should PRIMARILY:
options: Provide detailed technical specifications
Summarize health against scope, schedule, cost, and key risks, and present decisions required from the committee
Read through the full project plan line by line
Distribute the full risk register for committee review
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Summarize health against scope, schedule, cost, and key risks, and present decisions required from the committee

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project manager calculates a schedule variance of +$20,000 and a cost variance of -$30,000. What does this tell the project manager?
options: The project is ahead of schedule and under budget
The project is behind schedule and under budget
The project is ahead of schedule but over budget
The project is behind schedule and over budget
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: The project is ahead of schedule but over budget

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: Which factor MOST directly determines whether a task is on the critical path?
options: The task has the highest budget
The task has zero total float
The task is assigned to the project manager
The task has the most dependencies
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: The task has zero total float

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project manager is evaluating two project proposals. Project A has an NPV of $150,000 and Project B has an NPV of $200,000, but Project B carries significantly higher risk. The project manager should:
options: Select Project B because its NPV is higher
Select Project A because lower risk always wins
Evaluate both NPV and risk-adjusted return, then recommend based on organizational risk tolerance and strategic alignment
Select neither project and request new alternatives
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Evaluate both NPV and risk-adjusted return, then recommend based on organizational risk tolerance and strategic alignment

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project manager uses a probability and impact matrix. A risk rated 'High' probability and 'High' impact should be:
options: Accepted passively
Actively managed with a defined response strategy and owner assigned
Logged and reviewed quarterly
Transferred automatically to the sponsor
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Actively managed with a defined response strategy and owner assigned

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: Which scheduling technique adds buffer time at the end of the critical chain rather than within each task to protect against uncertainty?
options: Resource leveling
Critical Chain Method (CCM)
Fast tracking
Precedence diagramming
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Critical Chain Method (CCM)

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project is in execution when a new regulation takes effect that fundamentally changes a key deliverable's requirements. The project manager should FIRST:
options: Ignore the regulation until the project closes
Formally assess the impact on scope, schedule, cost, and risk; submit a change request; and present options to the change control board
Immediately redesign the deliverable without formal change control
Cancel the project
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Formally assess the impact on scope, schedule, cost, and risk; submit a change request; and present options to the change control board

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: What is the difference between 'quality control' and 'quality assurance' in project management?
options: Quality control is proactive; quality assurance is reactive
Quality assurance audits processes to ensure standards are followed; quality control inspects deliverables to detect and correct defects
They are identical activities with different names
Quality control applies only to construction projects
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Quality assurance audits processes to ensure standards are followed; quality control inspects deliverables to detect and correct defects

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project manager receives a change request that has already been implemented by a developer without approval. This situation is called:
options: Proactive change management
An unauthorized change or 'gold plating' – it must be retroactively assessed, documented, and either formally approved or reversed
A minor scope adjustment
A standard agile practice
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: An unauthorized change or 'gold plating' – it must be retroactively assessed, documented, and either formally approved or reversed

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: When preparing a project schedule, identifying task dependencies should occur:
options: After the schedule is baselined
Before estimating task durations to ensure the network logic is correct
After project execution begins
Only for tasks on the critical path
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Before estimating task durations to ensure the network logic is correct

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project manager documents that 'the financial system API will be available for integration by Week 6.' This is an example of:
options: A project constraint
A project assumption
A project risk
A project milestone
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: A project assumption

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: Which stakeholder register attribute is MOST useful for determining how frequently and in what format to communicate with a stakeholder?
options: Their title and department
Their level of interest, influence, engagement status, and preferred communication method
Their length of employment with the agency
Their budget authority
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Their level of interest, influence, engagement status, and preferred communication method

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A cost performance index (CPI) of 1.10 indicates:
options: The project is 10% over budget
The project is receiving $1.10 of work for every $1.00 spent – the project is under budget
The schedule is ahead by 10%
The team velocity is 10% above average
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: The project is receiving $1.10 of work for every $1.00 spent – the project is under budget

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project's contingency reserve is DIFFERENT from a management reserve in that contingency reserve is:
options: Controlled by executive leadership only
Planned for known risks within the project's risk register and is part of the project baseline
Available for any unplanned use without change control
Only used for vendor-related risks
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Planned for known risks within the project's risk register and is part of the project baseline

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: Which of the following BEST describes the 'Monitor and Control Project Work' process?
options: Creating the project schedule
Tracking, reviewing, and reporting project progress to meet performance objectives defined in the project management plan
Acquiring project team members
Conducting stakeholder interviews
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Tracking, reviewing, and reporting project progress to meet performance objectives defined in the project management plan

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project manager discovers that two team members have conflicting responsibilities on the same task in the RACI chart. This represents which type of planning deficiency?
options: Schedule compression error
Unclear roles and accountability creating a risk of ownership gaps or duplicate effort
Budget overrun
Scope creep
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Unclear roles and accountability creating a risk of ownership gaps or duplicate effort

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project charter's primary purpose is to:
options: Define detailed requirements for the final deliverable
Formally authorize the project, document high-level objectives, constraints, and assumptions, and empower the project manager
Replace the project management plan
Serve as the contract with the project sponsor
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Formally authorize the project, document high-level objectives, constraints, and assumptions, and empower the project manager

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project manager is asked to provide a 'parametric estimate.' This means the estimate is based on:
options: Team gut feeling
A statistical relationship between historical data and project variables (e.g., cost per function point)
A detailed bottom-up review of every task
Analogous estimation from a single past project
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: A statistical relationship between historical data and project variables (e.g., cost per function point)

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: Which document, produced during project closing, captures the final performance measurements and formally marks the project's completion?
options: Project charter
Final project report (project closure report)
Kickoff meeting agenda
Requirements traceability matrix
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Final project report (project closure report)

project_management_fundamentals_question.question: A project is 60% complete, has an SPI of 0.88, and has a hard statutory deadline. The MOST urgent action for the project manager is to:
options: Accept the delay
Conduct an immediate schedule impact analysis, identify recovery options (crash, fast track, or scope reduction), and present a recovery plan to the sponsor
Replace the project team
Submit a change request to extend the deadline
project_management_fundamentals_question.answer: Conduct an immediate schedule impact analysis, identify recovery options (crash, fast track, or scope reduction), and present a recovery plan to the sponsor
